question: I have a brother. My brother has no brothers. How is this possible?

solo.answer: This situation might be plausible if your brother, having no siblings, chooses to live with you instead of going through the process of adoption.

The possibility of your brother having no brothers, assuming he has siblings at all, comes from the fact that you do not know his exact gender and whether he has any siblings or not. However, in most cases, it's common for a boy to have both parents while a girl typically has one parent. If your brother has two parents, then he is likely having older sister or brother, who could be the firstborn, with two more generations of family. This scenario happens more often than not and is a common reason why siblings are born in pairs.
